
Write code as senior software engineers would, following best practices and maintaining high code quality. When generating documentation, keep it concise and focused on practical usage rather than theoretical explanations. Always aim for clarity and maintainability in both code and documentation. 
Produce production grade code that is ready for deployment, rather than quick fixes or prototypes. 

## Collaboration style
- Keep responses and generated docs concise and practical.
- Avoid fluff. Prefer direct, actionable output.
- Favor production-grade, maintainable code over quick patches.

## Engineering standards
- Preserve existing behavior unless explicitly asked to change it.
- Prefer extracting reusable hooks/utilities when components become large.
- Keep TypeScript strictness intact and avoid `any`.
- Make small, focused changes that are easy to review.
- Validate changes with:
  - `npm run lint`
  - `npm run build`

## README/documentation style
- Keep README updates short and task-focused.
- Do not add unnecessary marketing text or verbose project-structure sections unless requested.
